{{Infobox ancient site | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Tumulus
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | Alternate name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 130 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Burial facility = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | Excavated items = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Burial Mound) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}}} * {{Infobox ancient site | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Tumulus
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | Alternate name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 130 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Burial facility = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | Excavated items = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Burial Mound) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor Senka Mausoleum)] - Kashihara City [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]]

(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | Alternate name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 130 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Burial facility = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | Excavated items = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Burial Mound) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor Senka Mausoleum)] - Kashihara City [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Tumulus
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | Alternate name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 130 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Burial facility = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | Excavated items = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Burial Mound) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor {{Infobox ancient site | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Tumulus

While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Tumulus
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | Alternate name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 130 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Burial facility = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | Excavated items = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Burial Mound) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor Senka Mausoleum)] - Kashihara City [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Tumulus
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | Alternate name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 130 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Burial facility = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | Excavated items = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Burial Mound) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}}} * Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html {{Infobox ancient site | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Tumulus
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | Alternate name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 130 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Burial facility = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | Excavated items = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Burial Mound) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor Senka Mausoleum)] - Kashihara City [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Tumulus
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | Alternate name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 130 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Burial facility = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | Excavated items = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Burial Mound) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}}} * Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor {{Infobox ancient site | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Tumulus
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | Alternate name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 130 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Burial facility = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | Excavated items = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Burial Mound) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}}} * Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html (Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | Alternate name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 130 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Burial facility = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | Excavated items = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Burial Mound) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}}} * 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html (Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | Alternate name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 130 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Burial facility = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | Excavated items = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}}} * 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html {{Infobox ancient site | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Kofun
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | Alternate name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 130 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Burial facility = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | Excavated items = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}}} * 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html {{Infobox ancient site | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Kofun
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | Alternate name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} | Dimensions = Total length: 130 meters | Construction era = Early 6th century | Burial facility = Unknown | Interred individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | Excavated items = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | Map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == Dimensions == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}}} * Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor
