The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor Senka Mausoleum)] - Kashihara City {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Toriya Misanzai Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Toriya Misanzai Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Toriya Misanzai Kofun]] [[ja:鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]] [[sn:oriya Misanzai Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Kofun
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | alternate_name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Total length: 130 meters | built = Early 6th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | excavations = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor Senka Mausoleum)] - revision.comment: 26 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳 up to rev 99995441
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳 |画像 = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘
（右に前方部、左に後円部、手前に鳥屋池） |別名 = 鳥屋見三才古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町字見三才{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}} |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 28|緯度秒 = 41.71 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 47.83 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長130[[メートル|m]] |築造年代 = [[6世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第28代[[宣化天皇]]・[[橘仲皇女]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |ラベル = 鳥屋
ミサンザイ
古墳 |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北北東 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[宣化天皇]]・[[橘仲皇女]]
身狭桃花鳥坂上陵 拝所}}]] '''鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳'''（とりやミサンザイこふん、鳥屋見三才古墳{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}}）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''身狭桃花鳥坂上陵'''（むさのつきさかのえのみささぎ、身狹桃花鳥坂上陵）」として第28代[[宣化天皇]]・[[皇后]][[橘仲皇女]]の合葬陵に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]南縁、貝吹山北麓の尾根先端部において丘尾を切断して築造された古墳である{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社）|1981年}}。「ミサンザイ（見三才）」は「ミササギ（陵）」の転訛{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}}。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の天皇陵として同庁の管理下にあるが、これまでに整備工事の事前調査として同庁による調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北北東方に向ける{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社）|1981年}}。墳丘1段目のくびれ部両側には[[造出]]を付すほか、前方部東側にも張出状施設を有する{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]（[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪）が検出されている{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}}。墳丘周囲には盾形の周濠が巡らされるが、後世の[[ため池]]（鳥屋池）工事に際して大きく改変を受けている{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。埋葬施設・副葬品は明らかでない{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 この鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期前半の[[6世紀]]前半頃{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html 鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（宣化天皇陵）]（橿原市「かしはら探訪ナビ」）。（または中期後半の5世紀後半頃{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}}{{Sfn|宣化天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}）の築造と推定される。当時としては大和で最大級の古墳とされる{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、前述のように現在は[[宮内庁]]により第28代[[宣化天皇]]・[[橘仲皇女]]の合葬陵に治定されている{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。本古墳の南方には[[桝山古墳]]（[[倭彦命]] 身狭桃花鳥坂墓）が、西方には[[新沢千塚古墳群]]があり、それらとの関係性も指摘される{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[寛永]]年間（[[1624年|1624]]-[[1644年]]）、[[灌漑]]用ため池（鳥屋池）工事に伴う周濠の改変{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}。 * [[元禄]]12年（[[1699年]]）4月、元禄の探陵に際して[[宣化天皇]]陵に治定{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}。 * [[1971年]]度（[[昭和]]45年度）、整備工事に伴う事前調査（[[宮内庁書陵部]]）。 * [[1988年]]度（昭和63年度）、整備工事に伴う事前調査（宮内庁書陵部）。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:white;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" !colspan=2| !『続日本古墳大辞典』{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}} !『奈良県の地名』{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}} |- !colspan=2|墳丘長 |130[[メートル|m]]||140m |- !rowspan=2|後円部 !直径 |83m||83m |- !高さ |18.5m||16m |- !rowspan=2|前方部 !幅 |78m||78m |- !高さ |19.5m||16m |} なお、『日本古墳大辞典』および橿原市サイトでは墳丘長を138[[メートル]]とする{{Sfn|宣化天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}{{R|橿原市}}。 == 被葬者 == 鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第28代'''[[宣化天皇]]'''および[[皇后]]の'''[[橘仲皇女]]'''（たちばなのなかつひめみこ）の合葬陵に治定している{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/11}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、[[国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]]）11コマ。『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、[[学生社]]、2014年、p. 406。。宣化天皇について、『[[日本書紀]]』では宣化天皇4年（[[539年]]?）2月『日本書紀』宣化天皇4年（539年?）2月甲午（10日）条。に崩御し、宣化天皇4年11月『日本書紀』宣化天皇4年（539年?）11月丙寅（17日）条。に「大倭国身狭桃花鳥坂上陵」に葬られたとし、皇后の橘皇女およびその子もこれに合葬したという{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では宣化陵は遠陵の「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵」として記載され、[[大和国]][[高市郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西2町・南北2町で守戸5烟を毎年あてるとするが、合葬の旨の記載はない{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}。その後、宣化陵の所在に関する所伝は失われ、[[江戸時代]]の[[元禄]]の探陵に際し、元禄12年（[[1699年]]）4月に[[京都所司代]]の[[松平信庸 (篠山藩主)|松平信庸]]によって現陵に治定された{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''原典''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=[[直木孝次郎]]「宣化天皇」|ref={{Harvid|宣化天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵」（宣化天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=身狭桃花鳥坂上陵|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4-582-49030-1|ref={{Harvid|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社）|1981年}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=伊藤勇輔|year=1989|chapter=宣化天皇陵古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4-490-10260-7|ref={{Harvid|宣化天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=卜部行弘|year=2002|chapter=鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4-490-10599-1|ref={{Harvid|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1990|chapter=|title={{PDFlink|[https://shoryobu.kunaicho.go.jp/Publication/PDF/000/kiyo041a086.pdf 書陵部紀要 第41号]}}|publisher=[[宮内庁書陵部]]|isbn=|ref=}} - リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 ** 「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵整備工事箇所の調査」、「昭和45年度 身狭桃花鳥坂上陵整備工事に伴う事前調査」。 == 関連項目 == * [[宣化天皇]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]] to [[Toriya Misanzai Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳 |画像 = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]]
墳丘
（右に前方部、左に後円部、手前に鳥屋池） |別名 = 鳥屋見三才古墳 |所属 = |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町字見三才{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}} |緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 28|緯度秒 = 41.71 |経度度 =135|経度分 = 46|経度秒 = 47.83 |ISO = JP-29 |形状 = [[前方後円墳]] |規模 = 墳丘長130[[メートル|m]] |築造年代 = [[6世紀]]前半 |埋葬施設 = 不明 |被葬者 = （[[宮内庁]]治定）第28代[[宣化天皇]]・[[橘仲皇女]] |出土品 = [[埴輪]]片 |陵墓 = [[宮内庁]]治定「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵」 |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Nara |ラベル = 鳥屋
ミサンザイ
古墳 |アイコン = 前方後円墳-北北東 |ラベル位置 = top }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|[[宣化天皇]]・[[橘仲皇女]]
身狭桃花鳥坂上陵 拝所}}]] '''鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳'''（とりやミサンザイこふん、鳥屋見三才古墳{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}}）は、[[奈良県]][[橿原市]]鳥屋町にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[前方後円墳]]。 実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]により「'''身狭桃花鳥坂上陵'''（むさのつきさかのえのみささぎ、身狹桃花鳥坂上陵）」として第28代[[宣化天皇]]・[[皇后]][[橘仲皇女]]の合葬陵に治定されている。 == 概要 == [[奈良盆地]]南縁、貝吹山北麓の尾根先端部において丘尾を切断して築造された古墳である{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社）|1981年}}。「ミサンザイ（見三才）」は「ミササギ（陵）」の転訛{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}}。現在は[[宮内庁]]治定の天皇陵として同庁の管理下にあるが、これまでに整備工事の事前調査として同庁による調査が実施されている。 墳形は前方後円形で、前方部を北北東方に向ける{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。墳丘は2段築成{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社）|1981年}}。墳丘1段目のくびれ部両側には[[造出]]を付すほか、前方部東側にも張出状施設を有する{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}}。墳丘外表では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]（[[円筒埴輪]]・朝顔形埴輪）が検出されている{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}}。墳丘周囲には盾形の周濠が巡らされるが、後世の[[ため池]]（鳥屋池）工事に際して大きく改変を受けている{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。埋葬施設・副葬品は明らかでない{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 この鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期前半の[[6世紀]]前半頃{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}[http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html 鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（宣化天皇陵）]（橿原市「かしはら探訪ナビ」）。（または中期後半の5世紀後半頃{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}}{{Sfn|宣化天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}）の築造と推定される。当時としては大和で最大級の古墳とされる{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。被葬者は明らかでないが、前述のように現在は[[宮内庁]]により第28代[[宣化天皇]]・[[橘仲皇女]]の合葬陵に治定されている{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。本古墳の南方には[[桝山古墳]]（[[倭彦命]] 身狭桃花鳥坂墓）が、西方には[[新沢千塚古墳群]]があり、それらとの関係性も指摘される{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[寛永]]年間（[[1624年|1624]]-[[1644年]]）、[[灌漑]]用ため池（鳥屋池）工事に伴う周濠の改変{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}。 * [[元禄]]12年（[[1699年]]）4月、元禄の探陵に際して[[宣化天皇]]陵に治定{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}。 * [[1971年]]度（[[昭和]]45年度）、整備工事に伴う事前調査（[[宮内庁書陵部]]）。 * [[1988年]]度（昭和63年度）、整備工事に伴う事前調査（宮内庁書陵部）。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘の規模は次の通り。 {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center;background:white;white-space:nowrap;font-size:85%" !colspan=2| !『続日本古墳大辞典』{{Sfn|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}} !『奈良県の地名』{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}} |- !colspan=2|墳丘長 |130[[メートル|m]]||140m |- !rowspan=2|後円部 !直径 |83m||83m |- !高さ |18.5m||16m |- !rowspan=2|前方部 !幅 |78m||78m |- !高さ |19.5m||16m |} なお、『日本古墳大辞典』および橿原市サイトでは墳丘長を138[[メートル]]とする{{Sfn|宣化天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}{{R|橿原市}}。 == 被葬者 == 鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳の実際の被葬者は明らかでないが、[[宮内庁]]では第28代'''[[宣化天皇]]'''および[[皇后]]の'''[[橘仲皇女]]'''（たちばなのなかつひめみこ）の合葬陵に治定している{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}[https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/successive_list.html 天皇陵]（宮内庁）。宮内省諸陵寮編[{{NDLDC|1237123/11}} 『陵墓要覧』]（1934年、[[国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション]]）11コマ。『陵墓地形図集成 縮小版』 宮内庁書陵部陵墓課編、[[学生社]]、2014年、p. 406。。宣化天皇について、『[[日本書紀]]』では宣化天皇4年（[[539年]]?）2月『日本書紀』宣化天皇4年（539年?）2月甲午（10日）条。に崩御し、宣化天皇4年11月『日本書紀』宣化天皇4年（539年?）11月丙寅（17日）条。に「大倭国身狭桃花鳥坂上陵」に葬られたとし、皇后の橘皇女およびその子もこれに合葬したという{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}。『[[延喜式]]』[[諸陵式|諸陵寮]]『延喜式』巻21（治部省）諸陵寮条。では宣化陵は遠陵の「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵」として記載され、[[大和国]][[高市郡]]の所在で、兆域は東西2町・南北2町で守戸5烟を毎年あてるとするが、合葬の旨の記載はない{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}。その後、宣化陵の所在に関する所伝は失われ、[[江戸時代]]の[[元禄]]の探陵に際し、元禄12年（[[1699年]]）4月に[[京都所司代]]の[[松平信庸 (篠山藩主)|松平信庸]]によって現陵に治定された{{Sfn|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} '''原典''' {{reflist|group="原"}} '''出典''' {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=|chapter=|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=[[直木孝次郎]]「宣化天皇」|ref={{Harvid|宣化天皇（国史）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=石田茂輔「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵」（宣化天皇項目内）|ref={{Harvid|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（国史）}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=身狭桃花鳥坂上陵|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 30 奈良県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4-582-49030-1|ref={{Harvid|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社）|1981年}}}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=刊行後版（[[ジャパンナレッジ]]収録）、2006年|ref={{Harvid|身狭桃花鳥坂上陵（平凡社、刊行後版）|2006年}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=伊藤勇輔|year=1989|chapter=宣化天皇陵古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4-490-10260-7|ref={{Harvid|宣化天皇陵古墳（古墳）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=卜部行弘|year=2002|chapter=鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典|続 日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4-490-10599-1|ref={{Harvid|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳（続古墳）|2002年}}}} == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} リンクは宮内庁「書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム」。 ** 「身狭桃花鳥坂上陵整備工事箇所の調査」、「昭和45年度 身狭桃花鳥坂上陵整備工事に伴う事前調査」。 == 関連項目 == * [[宣化天皇]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Kofun
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | alternate_name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Total length: 130 meters | built = Early 6th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | excavations = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - (Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | alternate_name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Total length: 130 meters | built = Early 6th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | excavations = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - (Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | alternate_name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Total length: 130 meters | built = Early 6th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | excavations = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor Senka Mausoleum)] - Kashihara City {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|version=99995441|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Toriya Misanzai Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Toriya Misanzai Kofun]]}} [[sn:Toriya Misanzai Kofun]] [[ja:鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]] [[sn:oriya Misanzai Kofun]]

Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|qq=}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heibonsha|qq=}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Kofun
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | alternate_name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | area = Total length: 130 meters | built = Early 6th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | excavations = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanei era|qq=}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|qq=}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heibonsha|qq=}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor Senka Mausoleum)] - Kashihara City {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|version=99995441|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Toriya Misanzai Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Toriya Misanzai Kofun]]}} [[sn:Toriya Misanzai Kofun]] [[ja:鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]] [[sn:oriya Misanzai Kofun]]

The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|qq=}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heibonsha|qq=}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px|Kofun
(Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | alternate_name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} | area = Total length: 130 meters | built = Early 6th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | excavations = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/keyhole-shaped tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|ja|橿原市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashihara, Nara|qq=}}, Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|ja|宣化天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Senka|qq=}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|lt=Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|lt=Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|ja|新沢千塚古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|qq=}}, suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the {{ill|Kanei era|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanei era|qq=}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|ja|宮内庁書陵部|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Archives and Mausolea Department|qq=}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|qq=}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heibonsha|qq=}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|ja|宮内庁|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Household Agency|qq=}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor Senka Mausoleum)] - Kashihara City {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|version=99995441|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Toriya Misanzai Kofun]]}} [[sn:Toriya Misanzai Kofun]] [[ja:鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]] [[sn:oriya Misanzai Kofun]]

While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster]], suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei era]] (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Imperial Household Agency Archives]]. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! (Front mound on the right, rear mound on the left, Toriya Pond in the foreground)]] | alternate_name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture, Toriya Town, Misanzai district | coordinates = {{Coord|34|28|41.71|N|135|46|47.83|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 130 meters | built = Early 6th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | excavations = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} }} [[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture. While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. == Overview == The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency. The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond. The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster]], suggest a potential historical relationship. == History == * During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei era]] (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Imperial Household Agency Archives]]. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives. == area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters. == Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually. == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|qq=}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|qq=}}: Nara|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|qq=}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|qq=}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor Senka Mausoleum)] - Kashihara City {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳|version=99995441|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kashihara, Nara]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Imperial mausolea in Japan]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Toriya Misanzai Kofun]]}} [[sn:Toriya Misanzai Kofun]] [[ja:鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳]] [[sn:oriya Misanzai Kofun]] [[Category:奈良県の古墳]]
