== Overview ==
The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency.
The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond.
The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster]], suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei era]] (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Imperial Household Agency Archives]]. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.

[[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture.
While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime.
== Overview ==
The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency.
The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond.
The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster]], suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei era]] (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Imperial Household Agency Archives]]. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.

The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.

[[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture.
While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime.
== Overview ==
The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency.
The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond.
The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster]], suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei era]] (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Imperial Household Agency Archives]]. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg | alternate_name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.478253|N|135.779953|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 130 meters | built = Early 6th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | excavations = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728}}; {{ill|Tachibana no Nakatsu|WD=Q11121925}} }}
[[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture.
While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime.
== Overview ==
The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency.
The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond.
The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster]], suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei era]] (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Imperial Household Agency Archives]]. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.

Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg | alternate_name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.478253|N|135.779953|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 130 meters | built = Early 6th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | excavations = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728}}; {{ill|Tachibana no Nakatsu|WD=Q11121925}} }}
[[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture.
While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime.
== Overview ==
The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency.
The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond.
The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster]], suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei era]] (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Imperial Household Agency Archives]]. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.

[[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture.
While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime.
== Overview ==
The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency.
The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond.
The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster]], suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei era]] (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Imperial Household Agency Archives]]. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.

The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster]], suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei era]] (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Imperial Household Agency Archives]]. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.

[[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture.
While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime.
== Overview ==
The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency.
The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond.
The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster]], suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei era]] (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Imperial Household Agency Archives]]. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg | alternate_name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.478253|N|135.779953|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | area = Total length: 130 meters | built = Early 6th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = ([[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designation) [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | excavations = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728}}; {{ill|Tachibana no Nakatsu|WD=Q11121925}} }}
[[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Kashihara, Nara|Kashihara, Nara]], Nara Prefecture.
While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime.
== Overview ==
The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency.
The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond.
The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to [[:en:Emperor Senka|Emperor Senka]] and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the [[:en:Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster]], suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the [[:en:Kan'ei|Kanei era]] (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the [[:en:Archives and Mausolea Department|Imperial Household Agency Archives]]. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the [[:en:Imperial Household Agency|Imperial Household Agency]] designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=Kokushi Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}}: Nara|publisher=[[:en:Heibonsha|Heibonsha]]|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}}

The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11502665|lt=Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster}}, suggest a potential historical relationship.

Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg | alternate_name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.478253|N|135.779953|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} | area = Total length: 130 meters | built = Early 6th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | excavations = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728}}; {{ill|Tachibana no Nakatsu|WD=Q11121925}} }}
[[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture.
While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime.
== Overview ==
The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency.
The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond.
The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11502665|lt=Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster}}, suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kanei era}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|WD=Q4346533|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.
== References == {{Reflist}}

== Overview ==
The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency.
The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond.
The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11502665|lt=Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster}}, suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kanei era}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|WD=Q4346533|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg | alternate_name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.478253|N|135.779953|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Total length: 130 meters | built = Early 6th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | excavations = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}; {{ill|Tachibana no Nakatsu|WD=Q11121925|1=Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu}} }}
[[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture.
While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime.
== Overview ==
The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency.
The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond.
The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11502665|lt=Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|1=Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}, suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kanei era|1=Kan'ei}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|WD=Q4346533|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | image = File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, zenkei.jpg | alternate_name = Toriya Misanzai Kofun | location = {{ill|Kashihara|WD=Q496319|1=Kashihara, Nara}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.478253|N|135.779953|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Total length: 130 meters | built = Early 6th century | Burial Facility = Unknown | Interred_individual = ({{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designation) {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime | excavations = Haniwa fragments | Mausoleum = Designated by the Imperial Household Agency as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵) | map = {{location map|Japan Nara|lat_deg=34|lat_min=28|lat_sec=41.71|lon_deg=135|lon_min=46|lon_sec=47.83|label=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|mark=前方後円墳-北北東|label_position=top|width=200|caption=location in Nara Prefecture}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | commemorates = {{ill|Senka|WD=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}}; {{ill|Tachibana no Nakatsu|WD=Q11121925|1=Princess Tachibana no Nakatsu}} }}
[[File:Toriya Misanzai Kofun, haisho.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime]] '''Toriya Misanzai Kofun''' (鳥屋ミサンザイ古墳, Toriya Misanzai Kofun) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in {{ill|Kashihara, Nara|en|Kashihara, Nara|WD=Q496319|lt=Kashihara, Nara|1=Kashihara, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture.
While the actual interred individuals are unknown, the tumulus has been designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} as "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" (身狭桃花鳥坂上陵), the joint mausoleum of {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime.
== Overview ==
The tumulus is located at the northern foot of the Kaifuki Mountain ridge in the southern Nara Basin. It was constructed by cutting off the tip of a hill ridge. The name "Misanzai" is considered a dialectal variation of "Misasagi" (陵). Currently, the tumulus is managed by the Imperial Household Agency as a designated imperial mausoleum. Investigations have been conducted prior to restoration projects by the agency.
The tumulus has a keyhole shape, with the front mound oriented towards the north-northeast. The mound is constructed in two tiers, with additional projections on both sides of the constricted area of the first tier and a protrusion-like structure on the east side of the front mound. Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond.
The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11502665|lt=Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|1=Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}, suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kanei era|1=Kan'ei}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|WD=Q4346533|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=|title={{ill|Kokushi Daijiten|ja|国史大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Daijiten|1=National History Encyclopedia|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6426904}}|publisher={{ill|Yoshikawa Kobunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kobunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Naoki Kojiro|year=1981|chapter=Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi|title={{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}}: Nara|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|WD=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4-582-49030-1}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Urabe Yukihiro|year=2002|chapter=Toriya Misanzai Kofun|title={{ill|Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|続 日本古墳大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135524461|1=Revised Nihon Kofun Daijiten|2=ja|3=続日本古墳大辞典|WD=Q135524461}}|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyodo Shuppan|1=Tōkyōdō Shuppan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11524645}}|isbn=4-490-10599-1}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Toriya Misanzai Kofun}} * [https://www.kunaicho.go.jp/ryobo/guide/028/index.html Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi] - {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} * [http://www.city.kashihara.nara.jp/kankou/own_bunkazai/bunkazai/spot/toriya_minzai.html Toriya Misanzai Kofun (Emperor Senka Mausoleum)] - Kashihara City

Haniwa, including cylindrical and morning glory-shaped types, and fukiishi (covering stones) have been found on the surface of the mound. A shield-shaped moat surrounds the tumulus, though it has been significantly altered by later construction of the irrigation pond Toriya Pond.
The Toriya Misanzai Kofun is estimated to have been built in the early 6th century during the first half of the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (or possibly the late 5th century, according to some sources). It was one of the largest tumuli in the Yamato region at that time. While the interred individuals are unknown, the Imperial Household Agency attributes it to {{ill|Emperor Senka|en|Emperor Senka|WD=Q329728|lt=Emperor Senka|1=Emperor Senka}} and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. Nearby tumuli, such as {{ill|Masuyama Kofun|ja|桝山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuyama Kofun|qq=}} (attributed to Yamato-hiko no Mikoto) and the {{ill|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|en|Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11502665|lt=Shinzan Tsuzuka Kofun Cluster|1=Niizawa Senzuka Kofun Cluster}}, suggest a potential historical relationship.
== History == * During the {{ill|Kan'ei|en|Kan'ei|WD=Q1192913|lt=Kanei era|1=Kan'ei}} (1624–1644): Construction of the Toriya irrigation pond altered the surrounding moat. * Genroku 12 (1699): Designated as the mausoleum of Emperor Senka during the Genroku imperial mausoleum investigations. * 1971: Pre-restoration survey conducted by the {{ill|Archives and Mausolea Department|en|Archives and Mausolea Department|WD=Q4346533|lt=Imperial Household Agency Archives|1=Archives and Mausolea Department}}. * 1988: Additional survey conducted by the Imperial Household Agency Archives.
== area == The tumulus measures as follows: {| class="wikitable" style="text-align:center; background:white; white-space:nowrap; font-size:85%" ! colspan=2| ! 『Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten』 (2002) ! 『Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei: Nara』 (2006) |- ! colspan=2| Total length | 130 meters || 140 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Rear mound ! Diameter | 83 meters || 83 meters |- ! Height | 18.5 meters || 16 meters |- ! rowspan=2| Front mound ! Width | 78 meters || 78 meters |- ! Height | 19.5 meters || 16 meters |} Other sources, including the {{ill|Nihon Kofun Daijiten|ja|日本古墳大辞典|1=Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Kofun Daijiten|13=User:Immanuelle/Comprehensive Dictionary of Japanese Kofun|qq=}} and the Kashihara City website, estimate the length to be 138 meters.
== Interred Individuals == The actual interred individuals of the Toriya Misanzai Kofun are unknown. However, the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en|Imperial Household Agency|WD=Q1143602|lt=Imperial Household Agency|1=Imperial Household Agency}} designates it as the joint mausoleum of Emperor Senka and Empress Tachibana no Nakatsu Hime. According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|WD=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor Senka died in February of his fourth year of reign (539?) and was buried in "Misazumomoka Torisakaue no misasagi" in November of the same year. The empress and their child were later interred in the same mausoleum. The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.

The {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} describes the mausoleum as a distant imperial tomb located in Takichi District, Yamato Province, with a precinct of two by two chō (about 218 meters square) and five guards assigned annually.
